studyName: National Eye Institute (NEI) Age-Related Eye Disease Study 2 (AREDS2)

tables.description: This data table contains current medication information and includes information about medications taken to treat various conditions (angina, heart attack), and specific beta blocker medications (N=59 variables), angiotensin II converting enzyme inhibitors (N=60 variables), angiotensin receptor blockers (N=32 variables), anti-platelet medication (N=11 variables).

This data table contains the daily nutrient intake values calculated from responses to the Harvard Dietary Assessment (version Cherry New 96/97GP) using Harvard's nutrient analysis program. Estimates were generally calculated by first converting responses to each food item on the questionnaire into a food amount per day estimate. For example, if a respondent indicated they drank orange juice 1 time per week, and the reference serving size on the questionnaire was 6 ounces, the amount per day would be 0.86 ounces per day (6 ounces divided by 7 days=0.86 ounces per day).  Using this amount, nutrient values were then calculated for each food item using a food and nutrient database that included nutrient values for every item on the questionnaire. After daily nutrient intake values for every item on the questionnaire were calculated, the total daily intake of each nutrient was calculated by summing values across all food items on the questionnaire.

tables.description: This data table contains annual in-clinic visit information about both eyes and includes method of administration of dilated fundus exams, and statuses for: geographic atrophy, pigment abnormalities, treatment for age-related macular degeneration (AMD), drusen treatment, neovascularization or its consequences, AMD non-specific consequences, unknown treatments, laser photocoagulation, photodynamic therapy, anti-angiogenic therapy, anti-angiogenic medications and dosages, other FDA-approved therapy, other therapy, cataract surgery since last visit, Yag capsulotomy since last visit, refraction data, visual acuity scores, smoking status, diabetes history, adverse experience since last visit (and whether treatment was at hospital, or as outpatient), new lung diagnosis, supplement use (N=13 variables; new supplement, DHA, omega-3, cod liver oil, zeaxanthin, EPA, fish oil vitamin C, vitamin E, beta-carotene, zinc, copper), and Snellen visual acuity measurements.

variables.name: FDAODDT
variables.description: OTHER FDA APP TX DATE R

variables.name: FDAOSDT
variables.description: OTHER FDA APP TX DATE L

tables.description: This data table contains information from a cognitive telephone interview and includes responses from the Hearing Handicap Inventory for the Elderly (HHEI), the Wechsler Memory Scale (WECOK), CES-D Scale (DEPOK), Telephone Interview of Cognitive Status (TICS), and data from Letter Fluency tasks.

variables.name: NOIFANBU
variables.description: TICS NO IFS ANDS OR BUTS

tables.description: This data table contains information about eye treatments (cataract surgery and timing, Yag capsulotomy, treatment for age-related macular degeneration since last visit (drusen, geographic atrophy, corneal neovascularization, photodynamic therapy, anti-angiogenic therapy, medications, and dosages, or other therapies), and changes since last visit (new supplement, lutein, DHA, omega oil, cod oil, EPA, zeaxanthin, fish oil, vitamin C, vitamin E, beta-carotene, zinc, or copper).

This data table contains data from the Cognitive Telephone Interview (shortened) and includes responses from the Hearing Handicap Inventory for the Elderly (HHEI), CES-D Scale (DEPOK), and Telephone Interview of Cognitive Status (TICS).

variables.name: NOIFANBU
variables.description: TICS NO IFS ANDS OR BUTS

tables.description: This data table contains image lens grading data from the Reading Center and includes statuses for red reflex, iris pigmentation, iris neovascularization, lens status, lens opacities, cortical opacities, posterior subcapsular cataract (PSC), vacuoles, white anterior cortical opacities (WACOS), pseudo exfoliation, Mittendorf's dot, and other opacities.

variables.name: OVERDATE
variables.description: OVERRIDEDATE; blank values- Grade not overridden

variables.name: REVDATE
variables.description: REVIEWDATE; blank values- Grade not overridden

tables.description: This data table contains eye fundus grading information from the Reading Center (AMD Color version 1.0) and includes status for neovascular AMD, statuses for serous of fibrovascular pigment epithelial detachment (PED) presence and area measurements, serous retinal detachment and measurements, hard exudate characteristic of AMD, hemorrhage characteristic of AMD, laser photocoagulation treatment for neovascular AMD, subretinal fibrosis and area measurements, visible choroidal new vessels, pigment abnormalities, geographic atrophy, soft drusen, calcified drusen, reticular drusen, confounding and other abnormalities, and AREDS AMD calculated severity scale and level. For AMD Color 1.0 grading of fundus photos, two graders were used per visit; this was used for all qualification visits and some year 1 visits.

variables.name: SUPPA1
variables.description: Presence of abnormal autofluorescence (AF) > circle I2 or changes in background AF

variables.name: SUPPA3
variables.description: Decreased AF at the center of the macula

variables.name: SUPPA4
variables.description: Decreased AF with halo of increased AF

variables.name: SUPPB1
variables.description: Proximity of decreased AF to center (microns)

variables.name: SUPPC1
variables.description: Area of decreased AF within grid (DA)

tables.description: This data table contains eye fundus grading information from the Reading Center (AMD Color version 2.0) and includes status for neovascular AMD, statuses for serous of fibrovascular pigment epithelial detachment (PED) presence and area measurements, serous retinal detachment and measurements, hard exudate characteristic of AMD, hemorrhage characteristic of AMD, laser photocoagulation treatment for neovascular AMD, subretinal fibrosis and area measurements, visible choroidal new vessels, pigment abnormalities, geographic atrophy, soft drusen, calcified drusen, reticular drusen, confounding and other abnormalities, and AREDS AMD calculated severity scale and level. For AMD Color 2.0 grading of fundus photos, a single grader was used per visit; this was used for most year 1 visits and all visits after year 1.

variables.name: SUPPA1
variables.description: Presence of abnormal autofluorescence (AF) > circle I2 or changes in background AF

variables.name: SUPPA3
variables.description: Decreased AF at the center of the macula

variables.name: SUPPA4
variables.description: Decreased AF with halo of increased AF

variables.name: SUPPB1
variables.description: Proximity of decreased AF to center (microns)

variables.name: SUPPC1
variables.description: Area of decreased AF within grid (DA)

tables.description: This data table contains medical and family history and includes information about high blood pressure history (whether ever notified about by a medical professional, whether notified two or more times, taking medication), congestive heart failure (whether notified about by a medical professional, taking medication), coronary heart disease (whether notified about by a medical professional, taking medication), angina (whether notified about by a medical professional, taking medication for angina or chest pain), myocardial infarction (whether notified about by a medical professional, taking medication), stroke (whether notified about by a medical professional, taking medication), high cholesterol (whether notified about by a medical professional, taking medication), patient assessment of general health, relatives with macular degeneration, relatives with cataracts or cataract surgery, heart attack history of relatives and ages before or after 50, whether parents are deceased and age at death.

This table contains information about study closeout data including subject's lifestyle and environment including smoking status and exposure, medication use (blood modifying, gastrointestinal, heart, sleep and anxiety, and neuromuscular), visual acuity, diet, Charles Bonnet syndrome status, and location of grandparents' birth.

This data table contains information about protocol anomalies, including timing, photographs not obtained, blood draw not obtained, whether subject stopped taking supplements, intolerance to supplements (EPA/DHA, lutein/zeaxanthin, or AREDS type supplements [ATS]), supplement noncompliance (EPA/DHA, lutein/zeaxanthin, or ATS), supplements lost/stolen/thrown away, wrong bottle code, additional non-study supplements (nutrients, lutein/zeaxanthin, vitamin E, vitamin C, vitamin A, zinc, omega-3, fish oil, cod oil), and whether there were other protocol anomalies.

This data table contains vitamin usage data and includes the following medications with dosage information (N=39 variables; AREDS-type, multivitamin, Preservision, Vitamin A, beta-carotene, vitamin C, vitamin E, zinc, lutein/zeaxanthin, lutein, zeaxanthin, DHA, fish oil).

variables.name: LUZMGAM
variables.description: LUTEIN ZEAX MG AMT PER TA

variables.name: VITAIUAM
variables.description: VITAMIN A IU AMT PER TAB

variables.name: VITAREAM
variables.description: VITAMIN A RE AMT PER TAB

variables.name: VITEIUAM
variables.description: VITAMIN E IU AMT PER TAB

variables.name: VITEMGAM
variables.description: VITAMIN E MG AMT PER TAB

tables.description: This data table contains information about adverse events experienced by subject including type, existence prior to study enrollment, severity (life threating, medically significant), outcome (n= 5 variables; death, hospitalization, prolonged hospitalization, disability/capacity, resolution status), congenital or anatomical defect information, and information about timing of events.

This data table contains information about screening subject for inclusion/exclusion criteria and includes whether subject is aged 55-80, meets age-related macular degeneration (AMD) requirements, received pupillary dilation, will take study product, willing to stop taking other supplements, has a disease in either eye that would confound retinal assessment, previous retinal or other surgeries that might complicate assessment of AMD progression, chronic requirement for systemic or ocular medication known to be toxic to the retina/optic nerve, supplemented with 2 mg or more of lutein and/or 500 mg or more of DHA/EPA or fish oil for period of one year or more prior to date of randomization, intraocular pressure is more than 26 mm Hg or indication of glaucoma, cataract surgery within the last three months or capsulotomy within the last six weeks, lung cancer history, systemic disease with poor prognosis, status for hemochromatosis/Wilson's Disease/oxalate kidney stones, enrolled in another study that could affect adherence, taking a systemic anti-angiogenic for treatment for choroidal neovascularization or cancer, eligible for the Run-in AREDS-type supplements (ATS).

This data table contains hospitalization information and includes primary reason for hospitalization, events or procedures during hospitalization (heart attack or MI, coronary bypass, cardiac catheterization, balloon angioplasty, cardiac angioplasty/stent [PTCIHSP], cardioverter defibrillator placement, chest pain, stroke/transient ischemic attack, carotid artery angioplasty/stent/endarterectomy, peripheral artery angioplasty, congestive heart failure, resuscitated cardiac arrest, irregular heartbeat, and/or loss of consciousness).

This data table contains a cardiovascular outcomes study report and includes information about date of hospitalization, procedure, or death, type of event (cardio/cerebrovascular, myocardial infarction, irregular heartbeat, stroke/transient ischemic attack, resuscitated cardiac arrest, congestive heart failure, death, chest pain, other event), procedure (cardiovascular, cardiac catherization, carotid artery stent, coronary artery bypass, placement of implantable cardioverter defibrillator, cardiac angioplasty/stent, other procedure).

This data table contains information about enrolling subject into randomization and includes whether fundus photograph retakes were required for either eye, Run-In adherence information (for lutein/zeaxanthin and EPA/DHA), Run-in adherence percent (ineligible for study if less than 75%), willingness to stop taking supplements containing nutrients under the study, whether Reading Center assessed fundus photography of adequate quality, whether still eligible for AREDS2, status of advanced age-related macular degeneration, whether taking AREDS-type supplement before the qualification period, willingness to be randomized to one of the forms of the AREDS-type supplements, willingness and eligibility to take the "regular" form of the AREDS-type supplement (eligible if has not smoked within past year), whether agreed to participate in Nutritional Biochemistry Study in selected clinics, whether agreed to be contacted about the Cognitive Function Study, whether currently taking a multivitamin, and if wanted to take Centrum Silver.

This data table contains information about nutritional biochemistry including whether visit is for screening or post-randomization, visit number, timing of blood draw, timing of last food and drink, type of foods eaten, alcohol consumption, and whether subject smoked in the 24 hours prior.

This data table contains death report information and includes primary cause of death, location of death, whether death was related to study supplement, whether the death certificate was received, whether there was a report of a new adverse event since last study contact, and whether the subject was hospitalized since the last visit.

This data table contains outpatient procedure information and includes the following procedures: cardiac catheterization, cardiac angioplasty/stent (PTCIOPP), implantable cardioverter defibrillator, carotid artery angioplasty/stent/endarterectomy (CAANGIO), peripheral artery angioplasty, and whether there was an irregular heartbeat.

This data table contains a review of adverse events experienced by subject, including timing, outcome (determined to be serious, expedited or reportable), whether event is potentially related to study supplements, reporting requirements (whether DSMC, MedWatch, or expedited report required), whether event was expected or anticipated, and review status.

This data table contains information about death certificates received and includes ICD-9 Cause of Death Code, ICD-9 Code for the first, second, and third underlying causes of death, place of death, and manner of death.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.name: IN_AREDS_NO_ID
variables.description: Subject is from AREDS, phs000001, but Subject ID is not available

variables.name: SOURCE_SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID used in first source repository

variables.name: SOURCE_SUBJECT_ID2
variables.description: Subject ID used in second source repository

variables.name: SUBJECT_SOURCE
variables.description: First other source repository where subjects originate

variables.name: SUBJECT_SOURCE2
variables.description: Second other source repository where subjects originate

tables.description: This data table contains subject demographic information and includes age at randomization, gender, race, ethnicity, marital status, and highest level of school.
This data table contains reporting of serious adverse event information and includes date, age at event, and weight.

This data table contains study status information and includes whether subject completed study visits, reason for discontinuing study, whether subject was approached for closeout, and whether subject agreed to a closeout visit.
This data table contains information about subject enrollment into Advantage EDC (Electronic Data Capture) System and includes medication protocol assignments.
This data table contains Harvard Dietary Assessment information and includes whether assessment was obtained and assessment delivery format (self-administered or interviewed by clinical staff).

variables.name: MZ_TWIN_ID
variables.description: Twin ID for monozygotic twins and multiples. An MZ_TWIN_ID is not provided for dizygotic twins or multiples.

tables.description: This data table contains information about timing of patient re-randomization and reason for re-randomization, and new AREDS type supplement (ATS) assignment.
This sample attributes table includes body site where sample was collected, analyte type, and tumor status.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

tables.description: This data table contains Genetics Study informed consent information.

This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs and sample aliases. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.
